codeReview.summary: The Flet settings page covers the requested API fields, model loading dialog, connection testing, and delete-project confirmation flow, but the deletion path leaves the app mounted on a stale project view after the underlying project is removed.

issues.description: After `app_state.delete_project(proj_id)` succeeds, the handler only closes the dialog and shows a snackbar. It never triggers the app-level rebuild logic in `src/ui/app.py`, so the current settings tab and app bar can keep rendering the just-deleted project's name and controls until some unrelated navigation forces a refresh. This breaks the required delete-project flow because the UI does not reliably return to a valid post-deletion state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The reviewer instructions expect a repo-local skill file at `.factory/skills/<skillName>/SKILL.md`, but this repo has no such file for `scrutiny-feature-reviewer`, so the review had to proceed without being able to inspect the referenced skill guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Attempting to read `C:\exp\puzzle-solver\.factory\skills\scrutiny-feature-reviewer\SKILL.md` returned ENOENT during evidence gathering.

summary: Reviewed feature 2.3 via mission metadata, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit `1f3bfd1`. The implementation mostly matches the requested Flet settings UI, but the delete-project flow leaves the page in an invalid stale state after deletion, so the review fails.
